prevalence.value: 10-25% of hospitalized older adults; higher in ICU
prevalence.population: Hospitalized older adults in the EU
prevalence.source: European geriatric and ICU epidemiology syntheses

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: CAM/4AT screening with acute onset, inattention, and fluctuating course; identify underlying cause

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: variable but increasing in hospitals and ICUs
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate to high where prevention bundles are implemented

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Delirium screening integrated into many inpatient pathways
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Prevention bundles (mobilization, hydration, sleep, sensory aids), treat causes, minimize deliriogenic meds; antipsychotics only when necessary
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Implementation variability and staffing constraints in some hospitals
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong evidence base and structured hospital protocols in many member states
